title: Google OAuth2 Invalid Request Error
symptom: When attempting to authenticate with Google OAuth2, the user receives an 'Error 400: Invalid Request' message. This occurs after changes to the OAuth Redirect URL requirements.
not_this_if: Error occurs in a different environment
root_cause: The error arises because the OAuth Redirect URL is incorrectly set to an internal HTTP URL instead of the required external HTTPS URL. This is often due to misconfigured environment variables in the hosting setup.
fix_steps: Change the environment variable from `${SERVICE_FQDN_N8N}` to `${SERVICE_URL_N8N}` in your Docker Compose file.
Ensure that the Google OAuth settings include the correct HTTPS URL for your n8n instance.
Redeploy the n8n instance to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Update the Docker Compose file to use the correct environment variable for the OAuth Redirect URL and redeploy n8n.
